title: Macro Briefing — 2026-06-17
subtitle: Generated 08:50 PM ET on Wednesday, June 17, 2026
markdown:
# Macro Briefing — 2026-06-17
_Generated 08:50 PM ET on Wednesday, June 17, 2026_

## Market Summary
_LLM-augmented: false_

The Fed is in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING — front-end anchored, long-end driven by term premium and supply. With RESTRICTIVE_HOLD as the most probable next state (17%), the curve should remain flat to inverted while rates stay restrictive. 10y yield at 4.43 % yield; 2s10s at +29 bp. Front-end is anchored by Fed guidance; back-end takes its cue from supply, term premium and growth-data risk.

Curve bias: flattening pressure persists while the Fed holds. Fade 2s10s above +45 bp; receive 5s on a 50 bp dip.

No strong FX divergence signal from regime model.

No strong basis signal.

**Key levels (FRED):**
- Fed Target Range Lower: 3.50 % annualised (as of 2026-06-17)
- Fed Target Range Upper: 3.75 % annualised (as of 2026-06-17)
- Fed Funds Effective Rate: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-05-01)
- US CPI YoY: 4.17 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US Core CPI YoY: 2.82 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US PCE YoY: 3.77 % YoY (STALE, last update 2026-04-01)
- US Core PCE YoY: 3.29 % YoY (STALE, last update 2026-04-01)
- US Unemployment Rate: 4.30% (as of 2026-05-01)
- US 10Y Treasury Yield: 4.43 % yield (as of 2026-06-16)
- US 2Y Treasury Yield: 4.05 % yield (as of 2026-06-16)
- US 30Y Treasury Yield: 4.93 % yield (as of 2026-06-16)
- 10Y-2Y Spread: +29 bp (as of 2026-06-17)
- SOFR: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-06-16)
- ON RRP Take-up: $6.83bn (as of 2026-06-17)
- IORB: 3.65 % annualised (as of 2026-06-17)

## Central Bank Watch

**Fed:**
- Policy rate: 3.50% - 3.75% annualised target range (FRED DFEDTARL/U, as of 2026-06-17)
- Last meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- Next meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- _Analyst view:_ Restrictive hold with dovish drift; first cut delivered if labour softens through Aug NFP.
The Fed is actively tightening. Policy rate trajectory is upward — front-end bears, curve flattens. Terminal rate is approaching — any softening in guidance signals the pivot is near.
**Next move: cut/dovish** (17% probability). Hold probability: 75%. Terminal rate bias: lower than market.
Base case (75%): HAWKISH_TIGHTENING holds. Tail risk (17%): RESTRICTIVE_HOLD.
- Fed Funds Effective Rate: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-05-01)
- SOFR: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-06-16)
- Fed Target Range Upper: 3.75 % annualised (as of 2026-06-17)

**ECB:**
- Policy rate: [UNAVAILABLE — requires ECB stats endpoint]
- Last meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- Next meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- _Analyst view:_ End-of-cycle hold; balance of risks favours patience while wages cool.
2y rate diff vs USD at z = +1.47 — market positioning is dovish relative to USD.

**BOE:**
- Policy rate: [UNAVAILABLE — requires BOE stats endpoint]
- Last meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- Next meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- _Analyst view:_ Gradual easing with services CPI sticky; cuts come in measured 25 bp steps.
2y rate diff vs USD at z = -1.13 — market positioning is hawkish relative to USD.

**BOJ:**
- Policy rate: [UNAVAILABLE — requires BOJ stats endpoint]
- Last meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- Next meeting: [pending cb_calendar.yaml verification]
- _Analyst view:_ Cautious normalisation; FX weakness vs USD is the political/inflation pressure point.
2y rate diff vs USD at z = +0.70 — market positioning is dovish relative to USD.

**Balance Sheet / QT:** No fresh QT headlines today. Treasury runoff cap remains at $25bn/month per the Mar-25 slowdown; ECB APP redemptions ~€20bn/mo, PEPP fully reinvested through Dec-25.

**Relative Hawkishness Ranking (per-CB 2y rate-diff z vs USD baseline):**
1. **BOE** — terminal bias: hawkish vs USD (z=-1.13)
2. **Fed** — next move: cut/dovish; terminal bias: lower than market (rate-z baseline)
3. **BOJ** — terminal bias: modestly dovish vs USD (z=+0.70)
4. **ECB** — terminal bias: dovish vs USD (z=+1.47)

**Pairwise RV (USD rate differentials, z-score vs trailing 60obs):**
- USD-EUR: 2y diff z = +1.47 (USD-dovish stretched). RV expression: pay 2y EUR vs receive 2y USD if z drifts back to zero.
- USD-GBP: 2y diff z = -1.13 (USD-hawkish stretched). RV expression: receive 2y GBP vs pay 2y USD on extreme negative z; reverse on positive.
- USD-JPY: 2y diff z = +0.70 (in-range). RV expression: 2y USD-JPY rate diff carries USD/JPY spot — fade extreme z.

## Rates Market Assessment

**USD**: Currently in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING (84% confidence). Next move: **cut/dovish** (17% probability). Hold probability: 75%. Base case (75%): HAWKISH_TIGHTENING holds. Tail risk (17%): RESTRICTIVE_HOLD. Terminal rate bias: lower than market.

### Yield Curve & Term Premium
_LLM-augmented: false_
With the Fed in HAWKISH_TIGHTENING, the curve is under flattening pressure. Front-end is anchored by restrictive rates; long-end is driven by term premium and supply. Fade 2s10s above +45 bp; receive 5s on a 50 bp dip.
- US 2Y Treasury Yield: 4.05% (1d -2 bp, vs 2026-06-16)
- US 10Y Treasury Yield: 4.43% (1d -4 bp, vs 2026-06-16)
- US 30Y Treasury Yield: 4.93% (1d -4 bp, vs 2026-06-16)
- 2s10s Spread: +29 bp (1d -9 bp, vs 2026-06-17)
- US Real 10Y Yield (TIPS): 2.14 % yield (as of 2026-06-16)
- US 5Y5Y Forward Breakeven: 2.21% (as of 2026-06-17)

### SOFR Futures & Money Markets
With the Fed on hold/tightening, SOFR front-end is anchored near the target rate. Expect PCA dislocations in Reds/Greens/Blues for relative value — calendar spreads and flies are the cleanest expressions.
- Front-end OIS-implied path (FRED proxy, pending CME SR3 wiring): 1M 3.67 % yield | 3M 3.79 % yield | 6M 3.81 % yield | 1Y 3.84 % yield
- ON RRP take-up: $6.83bn (as of 2026-06-17)
- SOFR-IORB spread: -2.0 bp (SOFR 3.63% vs IORB 3.65%)
- Fed Funds Effective Rate: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-05-01)
- SOFR: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-06-16)

### Volatility Surface
- Realised 10y UST yield vol (20d ann): 72 bp/day. Without a live swaption feed we cannot quote 3m1y implied here; vol-of-vol watch keys off this realised number day-to-day.

### Swap Spreads & Funding
- _Spread levels pending DTCC SDR public-swap fetch (round-2 data item). Anchor view from 2024-26 distribution: 10y SS deeply negative (≈ -50 bp) on SLR-constrained dealer balance sheets and heavy coupon supply._

## FX Market Assessment

### G10 Spot & Positioning

**USD index tape:** AFE 112.56 index (as of 2026-06-12) | Broad 119.51 index (as of 2026-06-12)

**G10 FX tape:**
- EUR/USD 1.1573 (+0.5%, +58 pips) | 1w +0.3% | RV30d 5.2 | 52w pctile 26
- GBP/USD 1.3414 (+0.6%, +77 pips) | 1w +0.4% | RV30d 6.4 | 52w pctile 49
- USD/JPY 160.24 (-0.2%, -27 pips) | 1w -0.0% | RV30d 2.2 | 52w pctile 98
- AUD/USD 0.7047 (+0.7%, +52 pips) | 1w -0.2% | RV30d 8.8 | 52w pctile 74

**G10 FX Ranking (4-pillar model):**
1. **JPY** (+0.7): rates=+0.7, growth=+2.0, ToT=-0.3
2. **USD** (+0.3): rates=+0.0, growth=+1.1, ToT=+0.0
3. **EUR** (+0.1): rates=+1.5, growth=-1.5, ToT=-0.1
4. **GBP** (+0.0): rates=-1.1, growth=+1.8, ToT=-0.1
5. **AUD** (+0.0): rates=+1.1, growth=-2.0, ToT=+0.6
6. **CHF** (-0.0): rates=+0.0, growth=+0.0, ToT=-0.1
7. **CAD** (-0.2): rates=-1.4, growth=+0.7, ToT=+0.5

**Pairwise signals:**
- EUR/USD: **no bias** (signal: -0.15)
- GBP/USD: **no bias** (signal: -0.23)
- USD/JPY: **no bias** (signal: -0.41)
- AUD/USD: **no bias** (signal: -0.26)

### FX Volatility & Hedging Flows
- _ATM vol grid + 25-delta risk reversals pending CME FX-options settlement-file integration (round-2). Anchor view: JPY-side risk reversals priced for USD-call demand near BoJ rate-check thresholds._

### FX Carry & Forward Dynamics
- **EUR/USD**: 2y diff z = +1.47 (USD carry fading); position the carry leg accordingly.
- **CAD/USD**: 2y diff z = -1.35 (USD carry strong); position the carry leg accordingly.
- **GBP/USD**: 2y diff z = -1.13 (USD carry strong); position the carry leg accordingly.

**Carry-to-vol leaderboard (rate-z / RV30d, abs sorted):**
- USD/JPY: carry-to-vol = +0.31 (z=+0.70, RV30d=2.2%) — long-carry tilt
- EUR/USD: carry-to-vol = +0.28 (z=+1.47, RV30d=5.2%) — long-carry tilt
- GBP/USD: carry-to-vol = -0.18 (z=-1.13, RV30d=6.4%) — short-carry tilt
- AUD/USD: carry-to-vol = +0.13 (z=+1.11, RV30d=8.8%) — long-carry tilt

## Cross-Currency Basis

_Basis levels are 1y anchors pending DTCC SDR public-swap fetch (round-2). Action tag is derived from the live 2y rate-diff z-score against USD._

**ESTR/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +1.47.
- Anchor 1y basis: -22 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: ECB APP redemptions + Yankee / reverse-Yankee issuance pipeline (EIB/KfW)
- **bias: widen**

**SONIA/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = -1.13.
- Anchor 1y basis: -15 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: SONIA-SOFR rate gap + UK clearer USD funding demand at quarter-end
- **bias: tighten**

**TONAR/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +0.70.
- Anchor 1y basis: -42 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: Hedged JGB carry + MoF / Japanese lifer USD-asset hedge ratios
- **bias: widen**

**AONIA/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +1.11.
- Anchor 1y basis: -7 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: AONIA-SOFR rate gap + Aussie semi-government issuance hedging
- **bias: widen**

**SARON/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +0.00.
- Anchor 1y basis: -28 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: SNB sight-deposit dynamics + reverse-Yankee CHF→USD funding
- **bias: flat**

## Systematic Signal Context

**CB Balance Sheet Expansion/Contraction:**
- _No fresh QT headlines today. Working assumption: Fed at $25bn/mo Treasury runoff cap (Mar-25 slowdown); ECB APP redemptions ~€20bn/mo; BOJ holdings drifting flat as JGB sales pace remains slow._

**SOFR Front-End Slope:**
- SOFR: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-06-16)
- _Implication for 2Y basis: steep = easing priced = basis tightener_

**Swaption Vol (realised 10y proxy):**
- Realised 10y UST yield vol (20d ann): 72 bp/day — stand-in until swaption surface is wired.

**Composite Signals by Currency:**
- **EUR**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. EUR-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.
- **GBP**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. GBP-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.
- **JPY**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. JPY-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.
- **AUD**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. AUD-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.

**SOFR Curve RV:**
- _PCA residuals on the SOFR strip and 2s5s10s / 5s10s30s fly z-scores depend on CME SOFR-futures settles + DTCC SDR rate prints — both are round-2 data items. Use the FRED rate panel above for the directional view._

## Key Events Ahead

**CB Meetings:**
- 2026-06-19: BOJ MPM (Jun) [JPY]
- 2026-06-23: ECB Governing Council non-policy [EUR]

**Data Releases:**
- 2026-06-18: US Jobless Claims (weekly) [USD]
- 2026-06-19: BOJ press conference + Q&A [JPY]
- 2026-06-24: S&P Global Flash PMIs (US/EZ/UK) [global]
- 2026-06-26: US Core PCE deflator (May) [USD]
- 2026-06-27: UK Q1 GDP final [GBP]
- 2026-06-30: EZ Flash CPI (June) [EUR]
- 2026-07-02: US NFP (June) [USD]

**Treasury Auctions:**
- 2026-06-23: US 2y / 5y / 7y note auctions (week of) [USD]
- 2026-06-25: US 7y note auction [USD]

**CB Speakers:**
- 2026-06-25: Powell semi-annual testimony (Senate) [USD]
- 2026-06-26: Powell semi-annual testimony (House) [USD]

**From the RSS digest:**
- US CPI YoY: 4.17 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US Core CPI YoY: 2.82 % YoY (as of 2026-05-01)
- US PCE YoY: 3.77 % YoY (STALE, last update 2026-04-01)
- US Core PCE YoY: 3.29 % YoY (STALE, last update 2026-04-01)
- US Unemployment Rate: 4.30% (as of 2026-05-01)

## Trade Construction Context

_Each trade lists structure, entry/target/stop, conviction, horizon, carry/roll, sizing, rationale, catalyst, risks, and the explicit invalidating condition._

### Trade 1 — Receive 1y1y USD OIS (proxy: 2xDGS2 - DGS1) vs SR3 white-pack hedge

- **Instrument class:** rates_linear
- **Structure:** Receive 1y1y USD OIS at $10k DV01. Proxy entry computed live as 2*DGS2 - DGS1 = 2*4.05% - 3.84% = 4.26% (linear forward approximation valid at short tenors). Hedge: pay equivalent DV01 of the SR3 white-pack average (SR3 strip levels not in pipeline; size to plan, mark-to-market on entry).
- **Entry:** 1y1y OIS proxy at 4.26% (FRED DGS1 3.84% / DGS2 4.05%, as of 2026-06-16)
- **Target:** 4.01% (-25 bp from entry) as H2-26 cuts get priced more aggressively
- **Stop:** 4.46% (+20 bp from entry) — implies cuts get un-priced
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 6-10 weeks (through Aug FOMC)
- **Carry & roll:** +1.8 bp/m (1y1y rolls down the SOFR curve; live SR3 strip wiring required for exact roll)
- **Sizing:** $10k DV01 per leg, DV01-neutral. Budget $50k DV01.
- **Rationale:** Fed has held since May 2025 and labour is softening (UR drifting to 4.3%). The path Whites imply underestimates the risk that H2 data breaks the stay-on-hold consensus. The 1y1y point captures the FOMC reaction function cleanly without taking individual-meeting timing risk; the SR3 leg isolates the receive-the-cut-cycle convexity.
- **Catalyst:** Jul 24 NFP, Aug 12 CPI, Sep 17 FOMC
- **Risks:**
 - Hot July CPI re-prices the easing cycle out — front-end sells off.
 - Treasury supply spike forces term premium higher, dragging 1y1y up.
 - ON RRP collapses, funding tightness drags SOFR fixings higher.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** Powell explicit statement that the FOMC is biased to hold through year-end.

### Trade 2 — 2s10s UST curve steepener (live FRED-derived)

- **Instrument class:** rates_linear
- **Structure:** Receive 2y / Pay 10y, DV01-neutral. Entry computed live from FRED DGS2 (4.05%) and DGS10 (4.43%) as of 2026-06-16.
- **Entry:** 2s10s at +38 bp
- **Target:** +53 bp (+15 bp on trade)
- **Stop:** +28 bp (-10 bp on trade)
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 6-10 weeks
- **Carry & roll:** Roll-down depends on forwards; rough +0.3 bp/m at current curve shape
- **Sizing:** DV01-matched. $1bn 10y notional ≈ $850k DV01; budget $100k portfolio DV01.
- **Rationale:** 2s10s at +38 bp — in-range. Mild steepener bias on the working assumption that the next regime move is toward easing. Trade is constructed entirely from FRED constant-maturity yields, so all entry/target/stop levels are auditable end-to-end.
- **Catalyst:** Treasury QRA Jul 31; Aug NFP; Sep FOMC
- **Risks:**
 - Term-premium shock (long-end shoots wider) reverses the move.
 - Supply surprise at the August refunding cheapens 10s.
 - Risk-off flight-to-quality bull-flattens the curve.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** Spread breaches the stop in a single session on a known catalyst (NFP / FOMC / refunding).

### Trade 3 — Long USD/JPY topside via 3M 25-delta risk reversal

- **Instrument class:** fx_option
- **Structure:** 3M 25-delta risk reversal sized to 0.25% NAV vega. Entry spot is the live FRED DEXJPUS print (160.24, as of 2026-06-12); strikes set to 25-delta from spot. [RR vol: UNAVAILABLE - requires CME FX-options settlement file]
- **Entry:** Spot 160.24 (FRED DEXJPUS as of 2026-06-12); [RR vol: UNAVAILABLE - requires CME FX-options settlement file]
- **Target:** Spot 164.41 (+2.6% from entry); RR vol: re-mark at hedge desk
- **Stop:** Spot 157.20 (-1.9% from entry) or BoJ verbal intervention
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 3M to expiry
- **Carry & roll:** Spot has negative forward carry on USD/JPY; structure is theta-neutral at entry. Exact figures require CME settle file.
- **Sizing:** 0.25% NAV vega
- **Rationale:** BoJ rate-check threshold caps upside speed but the trend stays intact; rates-vol stays elevated. USD-call demand is the cleanest expression of continued JPY weakness without paying premium upfront. Entry anchored to today's FRED DEXJPUS at 160.24.
- **Catalyst:** Jun FOMC hawkish hold; BoJ Jul 31 status quo
- **Risks:**
 - MoF FX intervention (>$30bn print).
 - BoJ surprise hike >15 bp.
 - Sharp risk-off bid for JPY haven demand.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** BoJ surprise hike >15 bp or confirmed >$30bn MoF intervention print.

sections.title: Cross-Currency Basis
sections.markdown:
_Basis levels are 1y anchors pending DTCC SDR public-swap fetch (round-2). Action tag is derived from the live 2y rate-diff z-score against USD._

**ESTR/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +1.47.
- Anchor 1y basis: -22 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: ECB APP redemptions + Yankee / reverse-Yankee issuance pipeline (EIB/KfW)
- **bias: widen**

**SONIA/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = -1.13.
- Anchor 1y basis: -15 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: SONIA-SOFR rate gap + UK clearer USD funding demand at quarter-end
- **bias: tighten**

**TONAR/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +0.70.
- Anchor 1y basis: -42 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: Hedged JGB carry + MoF / Japanese lifer USD-asset hedge ratios
- **bias: widen**

**AONIA/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +1.11.
- Anchor 1y basis: -7 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: AONIA-SOFR rate gap + Aussie semi-government issuance hedging
- **bias: widen**

**SARON/SOFR:**
- 2y rate diff vs USD z = +0.00.
- Anchor 1y basis: -28 bp _anchor — DTCC fetch pending round-2_
- Driver: SNB sight-deposit dynamics + reverse-Yankee CHF→USD funding
- **bias: flat**

sections.title: Systematic Signal Context
sections.markdown:
**CB Balance Sheet Expansion/Contraction:**
- _No fresh QT headlines today. Working assumption: Fed at $25bn/mo Treasury runoff cap (Mar-25 slowdown); ECB APP redemptions ~€20bn/mo; BOJ holdings drifting flat as JGB sales pace remains slow._

**SOFR Front-End Slope:**
- SOFR: 3.63 % annualised (as of 2026-06-16)
- _Implication for 2Y basis: steep = easing priced = basis tightener_

**Swaption Vol (realised 10y proxy):**
- Realised 10y UST yield vol (20d ann): 72 bp/day — stand-in until swaption surface is wired.

**Composite Signals by Currency:**
- **EUR**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. EUR-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.
- **GBP**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. GBP-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.
- **JPY**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. JPY-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.
- **AUD**: 2Y signal = neutral basis; 10Y signal = neutral. Regime: Unknown. AUD-USD regime states not yet classified; basis signal driven by flow/seasonals.

**SOFR Curve RV:**
- _PCA residuals on the SOFR strip and 2s5s10s / 5s10s30s fly z-scores depend on CME SOFR-futures settles + DTCC SDR rate prints — both are round-2 data items. Use the FRED rate panel above for the directional view._

sections.title: Trade Construction Context
sections.markdown:
_Each trade lists structure, entry/target/stop, conviction, horizon, carry/roll, sizing, rationale, catalyst, risks, and the explicit invalidating condition._

### Trade 1 — Receive 1y1y USD OIS (proxy: 2xDGS2 - DGS1) vs SR3 white-pack hedge

- **Instrument class:** rates_linear
- **Structure:** Receive 1y1y USD OIS at $10k DV01. Proxy entry computed live as 2*DGS2 - DGS1 = 2*4.05% - 3.84% = 4.26% (linear forward approximation valid at short tenors). Hedge: pay equivalent DV01 of the SR3 white-pack average (SR3 strip levels not in pipeline; size to plan, mark-to-market on entry).
- **Entry:** 1y1y OIS proxy at 4.26% (FRED DGS1 3.84% / DGS2 4.05%, as of 2026-06-16)
- **Target:** 4.01% (-25 bp from entry) as H2-26 cuts get priced more aggressively
- **Stop:** 4.46% (+20 bp from entry) — implies cuts get un-priced
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 6-10 weeks (through Aug FOMC)
- **Carry & roll:** +1.8 bp/m (1y1y rolls down the SOFR curve; live SR3 strip wiring required for exact roll)
- **Sizing:** $10k DV01 per leg, DV01-neutral. Budget $50k DV01.
- **Rationale:** Fed has held since May 2025 and labour is softening (UR drifting to 4.3%). The path Whites imply underestimates the risk that H2 data breaks the stay-on-hold consensus. The 1y1y point captures the FOMC reaction function cleanly without taking individual-meeting timing risk; the SR3 leg isolates the receive-the-cut-cycle convexity.
- **Catalyst:** Jul 24 NFP, Aug 12 CPI, Sep 17 FOMC
- **Risks:**
 - Hot July CPI re-prices the easing cycle out — front-end sells off.
 - Treasury supply spike forces term premium higher, dragging 1y1y up.
 - ON RRP collapses, funding tightness drags SOFR fixings higher.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** Powell explicit statement that the FOMC is biased to hold through year-end.

### Trade 2 — 2s10s UST curve steepener (live FRED-derived)

- **Instrument class:** rates_linear
- **Structure:** Receive 2y / Pay 10y, DV01-neutral. Entry computed live from FRED DGS2 (4.05%) and DGS10 (4.43%) as of 2026-06-16.
- **Entry:** 2s10s at +38 bp
- **Target:** +53 bp (+15 bp on trade)
- **Stop:** +28 bp (-10 bp on trade)
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 6-10 weeks
- **Carry & roll:** Roll-down depends on forwards; rough +0.3 bp/m at current curve shape
- **Sizing:** DV01-matched. $1bn 10y notional ≈ $850k DV01; budget $100k portfolio DV01.
- **Rationale:** 2s10s at +38 bp — in-range. Mild steepener bias on the working assumption that the next regime move is toward easing. Trade is constructed entirely from FRED constant-maturity yields, so all entry/target/stop levels are auditable end-to-end.
- **Catalyst:** Treasury QRA Jul 31; Aug NFP; Sep FOMC
- **Risks:**
 - Term-premium shock (long-end shoots wider) reverses the move.
 - Supply surprise at the August refunding cheapens 10s.
 - Risk-off flight-to-quality bull-flattens the curve.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** Spread breaches the stop in a single session on a known catalyst (NFP / FOMC / refunding).

### Trade 3 — Long USD/JPY topside via 3M 25-delta risk reversal

- **Instrument class:** fx_option
- **Structure:** 3M 25-delta risk reversal sized to 0.25% NAV vega. Entry spot is the live FRED DEXJPUS print (160.24, as of 2026-06-12); strikes set to 25-delta from spot. [RR vol: UNAVAILABLE - requires CME FX-options settlement file]
- **Entry:** Spot 160.24 (FRED DEXJPUS as of 2026-06-12); [RR vol: UNAVAILABLE - requires CME FX-options settlement file]
- **Target:** Spot 164.41 (+2.6% from entry); RR vol: re-mark at hedge desk
- **Stop:** Spot 157.20 (-1.9% from entry) or BoJ verbal intervention
- **Conviction:** MED
- **Horizon:** 3M to expiry
- **Carry & roll:** Spot has negative forward carry on USD/JPY; structure is theta-neutral at entry. Exact figures require CME settle file.
- **Sizing:** 0.25% NAV vega
- **Rationale:** BoJ rate-check threshold caps upside speed but the trend stays intact; rates-vol stays elevated. USD-call demand is the cleanest expression of continued JPY weakness without paying premium upfront. Entry anchored to today's FRED DEXJPUS at 160.24.
- **Catalyst:** Jun FOMC hawkish hold; BoJ Jul 31 status quo
- **Risks:**
 - MoF FX intervention (>$30bn print).
 - BoJ surprise hike >15 bp.
 - Sharp risk-off bid for JPY haven demand.
- **Invalidates the thesis:** BoJ surprise hike >15 bp or confirmed >$30bn MoF intervention print.
